what: Provide the account of your station
title: Example of WISP.data functions
code: userid <- Sys.getenv("WISP_IREA_USERID") pwd <- Sys.getenv("WISP_IREA_PSW")

what: Get data from the WISP station server for a single or multiple dates.
title: Example of WISP.data functions
text: function save_csv parameter set TRUE, function save reflectance data .csv file ‘output’ folder within working directory.
code: reflect_data <- WISP.data::wisp_get_reflectance_multi_data(   time_from = "2024-09-11T09:00",   time_to   = "2024-09-17T14:00",   station   = "WISPstation012",   userid    = userid,   pwd       = pwd,   save_csv  = FALSE,   out_dir   = tempdir() )

what: Quality Check (QC) of the reflectance data
title: Example of WISP.data functions
text: function save_csv parameter set TRUE, function save quality checked reflectance data .csv file ‘output’ folder within working directory. User-configurable Parameters: maxPeak (numeric): maximum allowed reflectance peak threshold. Default: 0.05 maxPeak_blue (numeric): maximum allowed threshold blue region. Default: 0.02 qa_threshold (numeric): maximum threshold QA index. Default: 0.5 qwip_threshold (numeric): minimum threshold QWIP index. Default: 0.2 calc_scatt (logical): TRUE, computes scattering parameters band ratios calc_SPM (logical): TRUE, computes SPM concentration according Novoa et al. (2017) calc_TUR (logical): TRUE, computes TUR concentration according Novoa et al. (2017) calc_TSS (logical): TRUE, computes SPM concentration according Jiang et al. (2021) calc_gons (logical): TRUE, computes CHL concentration according Gons et al. (2002) calc_gons740 (logical): TRUE, computes CHL concentration according Gons et al. (2002) calc_NDCI (logical): TRUE, computes NDCI index according Mishra et al. (2012) calc_mishra (logical): TRUE, computes CHL concentration according Mishra et al. (2012) calc_dom_wave (logical): TRUE, computes dominant wavelength Hue Angle calc_OWT (logical): TRUE, computes Optical Water Type classification
code: reflect_data_qc <- WISP.data::wisp_qc_reflectance_data(   data           = reflect_data,   maxPeak        = 0.05,   maxPeak_blue   = 0.02,   qa_threshold   = 0.5,   qwip_threshold = 0.2,   calc_scatt     = TRUE,   calc_SPM       = TRUE,   calc_TUR       = TRUE,   calc_TSS       = TRUE,   calc_gons      = TRUE,   calc_gons740   = TRUE,   calc_NDCI      = TRUE,   calc_mishra    = TRUE,   calc_dom_wave  = TRUE,   calc_OWT       = TRUE,   save_csv       = FALSE,   out_dir        = tempdir() )  # ---- # 106 spectral signatures were removed during QC: #  # - 16 spectral signatures were removed thanks to QA+QWIP # - 2 spectral signatures were removed thanks to QC1+QA+QWIP # - 4 spectral signatures were removed thanks to QC2+QA+QWIP # - 1 spectral signatures were removed thanks to QC3+QC4+QA+QWIP # - 4 spectral signatures were removed thanks to QC4+QA+QWIP # - 79 spectral signatures were removed thanks to QC6+QA+QWIP #  # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 09:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 09:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 09:30:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 09:45:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 10:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 10:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 10:30:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 10:45:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 11:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 11:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QC2 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 11:30:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 11:45:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 12:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 12:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 12:30:06 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 12:45:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 13:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 13:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-11 13:30:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # ... # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 09:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QC3 QC4 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 09:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QC4 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 09:30:05 has been removed thanks to: QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 09:45:05 has been removed thanks to: QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 10:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 10:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 10:30:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 10:45:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 11:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 11:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 11:30:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 11:45:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 12:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 12:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 12:30:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 12:45:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 13:00:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 13:15:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 13:30:05 has been removed thanks to: QC4 QA QWIP # The spectral signature of 2024-09-17 13:45:05 has been removed thanks to: QC6 QA QWIP #  # QA remove spectral signatures with low quality based on Wei et al. (2016) # QC1 remove spectral signatures with negative values below 845 nm # QC2 remove spectral signatures with outliers in the NIR (840 nm > 700 nm) # QC3 remove spectral signatures with maximum peak greater than maxPeak # QC4 remove spectral signatures with outliers in the Blue domain # QC6 remove 'invalid' and 'None' spectral signatures according to level2.quality # QWIP remove spectral signatures with low quality based on Dierssen et al., 2022 # ----

what: Skyglint Removal (SR) of the reflectance data
title: Example of WISP.data functions
text: function save_csv parameter set TRUE, function save reflectance data sunglint removed .csv file ‘output’ folder within working directory. User-configurable Parameters: calc_scatt (logical): TRUE, computes scattering parameters band ratios calc_SPM (logical): TRUE, computes SPM concentration according Novoa et al. (2017) calc_TUR (logical): TRUE, computes TUR concentration according Novoa et al. (2017) calc_TSS (logical): TRUE, computes SPM concentration according Jiang et al. (2021) calc_gons (logical): TRUE, computes CHL concentration according Gons et al. (2002) calc_gons740 (logical): TRUE, computes CHL concentration according Gons et al. (2002) calc_NDCI (logical): TRUE, computes NDCI index according Mishra et al. (2012) calc_mishra (logical): TRUE, computes CHL concentration according Mishra et al. (2012) calc_dom_wave (logical): TRUE, computes dominant wavelength Hue Angle calc_OWT (logical): TRUE, computes Optical Water Type classification
code: reflect_data_sr <- WISP.data::wisp_sr_reflectance_data(   qc_data       = reflect_data_qc,   calc_scatt    = TRUE,   calc_SPM      = TRUE,   calc_TUR      = TRUE,   calc_TSS      = TRUE,   calc_gons     = TRUE,   calc_gons740  = TRUE,   calc_NDCI     = TRUE,   calc_mishra   = TRUE,   calc_dom_wave = TRUE,   calc_OWT      = TRUE,   save_csv      = FALSE,   out_dir       = tempdir() )

title: Example of WISP.data functions
text: function, can set TRUE parameters want display legend three plots (raw, QC, SR).
code: custom_raw <- list(   legend_TSM  = TRUE,    legend_Chla = TRUE,    legend_Kd   = TRUE,    legend_cpc  = TRUE )  custom_qc <- list(   legend_TSM            = TRUE,    legend_Chla           = TRUE,    legend_Kd             = TRUE,    legend_cpc            = TRUE,    legend_scatt          = TRUE,   legend_ratio          = TRUE,   legend_novoa_SPM      = TRUE,   legend_novoa_TUR      = TRUE,   legend_jiang_TSS      = TRUE,   legend_gons_CHL       = TRUE,   legend_gons740_CHL    = TRUE,   legend_NDCI           = TRUE,   legend_mishra_CHL     = TRUE,   legend_hue_angle      = TRUE,    legend_dom_wavelength = TRUE,   legend_OWT_class      = TRUE,   legend_OWT_score      = TRUE,   legend_OWT_z_dist     = TRUE )  custom_sr <- list(   legend_TSM            = TRUE,    legend_Chla           = TRUE,    legend_Kd             = TRUE,    legend_cpc            = TRUE,    legend_scatt          = TRUE,   legend_ratio          = TRUE,   legend_novoa_SPM      = TRUE,   legend_novoa_TUR      = TRUE,   legend_jiang_TSS      = TRUE,   legend_gons_CHL       = TRUE,   legend_gons740_CHL    = TRUE,   legend_NDCI           = TRUE,   legend_mishra_CHL     = TRUE,   legend_hue_angle      = TRUE,    legend_dom_wavelength = TRUE,   legend_OWT_class      = TRUE,   legend_OWT_score      = TRUE,   legend_OWT_z_dist     = TRUE )  fig_comparison <- WISP.data::wisp_plot_comparison(   raw_data = reflect_data,    qc_data  = reflect_data_qc,    sr_data  = reflect_data_sr,   raw_args = custom_raw,   qc_args  = custom_qc,   sr_args  = custom_sr ) fig_comparison

title: Example of WISP.data functions
text: function, can modify following variables: params (character vector): list parameters visualize none: displays individual measurements (ideal single-day analysis) daily_mean: calculates daily mean adds ribbon (standard deviation) daily_median: calculates daily median merge_plot (logical): TRUE, parameters sharing unit overlaid single plot
code: fig_trend <- WISP.data::wisp_trend_plot(   data       = reflect_data_sr,   params     = c("TSM", "Chla", "Novoa_SPM", "Mishra_CHL"),   aggregate  = "daily_mean",   merge_plot = TRUE ) ##  ## ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Parameters available (`params`): TSM, Chla, Kd, cpc, scatt, ratio, Novoa_SPM, Novoa_TUR, Jiang_TSS, Gons_CHL, Gons740_CHL, NDCI, Mishra_CHL, Hue_Angle, Dom_Wave ##  ## Aggregation methods (`aggregate`): ##  - 'none': Plot all available values (requires only one day in `wisp_get_reflectance_data()`). ##  - 'daily_mean': Plot the daily average and standard deviation (requires multiple days in `wisp_get_reflectance_data()`). ##  - 'daily_median': Plot the daily median (requires multiple days in `wisp_get_reflectance_data()`) ## ---------------------------------------------------------------------- fig_trend

title: Managing WISPstation Hyperspectral Data
text: WISP.data R package designed automate acquisition, quality control (QC), analysis, visualization spectral data collected WISPstation fixed spectroradiometer. Developed Water Insight evolution handheld WISP-3 system, WISPstation fixed instrument records radiance irradiance across wavelength range 350 1100 nm (spectral resolution 4.6 nm). package serves crucial bridge Water Insight API services research community, enabling transformation raw reflectance measurements scientifically validated, analysis-ready products fully reproducible transparent workflows. WISPstation operates 8 specialized channels optimize data collection: Dual-directional radiance: two sets sensors (NNW NNE) measure upwelling radiance (LuL_u) sky radiance (LskyL_{sky}) 40° angles. Smart orientation: system automatically selects best-oriented sensor set based Sun’s position, maintaining relative azimuth angle approximately 135°. Irradiance calibration: includes two downwelling irradiance (EsE_s) channels two unexposed “dark” channels assess sensor degradation time. primary aim WISP.data derivation Remote Sensing Reflectance (Rrs), defined ratio water-leaving radiance (LwL_w) downwelling irradiance (EsE_s): Rrs(λ)=Lw(λ)Es(λ)=Lu(λ)−ρ⋅Lsky(λ)Es(λ)\text{Rrs}(\lambda) = \frac{L_w(\lambda)}{E_s(\lambda)} = \frac{L_u(\lambda) - \rho \cdot L_{sky}(\lambda)}{E_s(\lambda)} package handles complex transition total upwelling radiance (LuL_u), includes unwanted sky-glint sun-glint, pure water-leaving signal (LwL_w) integrating LskyL_{sky} EsE_s measurements standardized atmospheric correction algorithms. WISP.data provides modular set R functions : downloading WISPstation data; performing automated reproducible quality control (QC); performing automated skyglint removal (SR); applying different algorithms spectral measurements; visualizing spectral derived data products. makes WISP.data ideal solution operational water quality monitoring, long-term research applications, integration large-scale environmental data pipelines.

title: Managing WISPstation Hyperspectral Data
text: WISPstation fixed spectrometer plays crucial role continuous monitoring water quality, providing high-frequency spectral measurements essential environmental observation, ecosystem assessment, long-term trend analysis. However, effective management scientific use spectral data present several significant challenges. Data retrieval API services often labor-intensive technically demanding, especially users without advanced programming experience. addition, raw spectral measurements sometimes affected radiometric problems making robust standardized quality control procedures indispensable. Without rigorous filtering validation protocols, derived products can unreliable scientifically misleading. critical barrier lies interpretation spectral signatures . non-expert users, difficult assess physical optical plausibility reflectance spectra, identify anomalous signals, distinguish instrument artifacts real environmental variability. Moreover, application third-party bio-optical algorithms estimation water quality products typically requires substantial domain knowledge, careful parameterization, consistent preprocessing workflows, rarely standardized across studies. WISP.data addresses challenges providing integrated, reproducible, user-oriented software ecosystem unifies data acquisition, quality control, spectral validation, product generation within single R-based framework. lowering technical methodological barriers, package enables expert non-expert users transform raw WISPstation measurements reliable, scientifically consistent water quality products, fostering reproducibility, comparability, broader adoption spectral monitoring technologies aquatic research operational monitoring.

title: Managing WISPstation Hyperspectral Data
text: can install development version WISP.data directly GitHub. following commands automatically install required dependencies:
code: # Install remotes if not already available if (!require("remotes")) install.packages("remotes")  # Install WISP.data and dependencies remotes::install_github("oggioniale/WISP.data", dependencies = TRUE)

Limnology Oceanography, 69(7), 1547-1561. https://doi.org/10.1002/lno.12606 Dierssen, H. M., Vandermeulen, R. ., Barnes, B. B., Castagna, ., Knaeps, E., & Vanhellemont, Q. (2022). what: Get data of reflectance (level2) from WISPstation for a specific date — wisp_get_reflectance_data
title: Get data of reflectance (level2) from WISPstation for a specific date — wisp_get_reflectance_data
text: function represents main entry point data acquisition within WISP.data package. connects official Water Insight APIs download spectral reflectance measurements user-defined time interval. function handles authentication queries remote server returns retrieved data structured tibble format. addition hyperspectral reflectance data (350–900 nm), function also retrieves water quality parameters natively computed WISPstation, including: TSM, Chla, Kd, cpc.

title: Get data of reflectance (level2) from WISPstation for a specific date — wisp_get_reflectance_data
code: wisp_get_reflectance_data(   version = "1.0",   time_from = NULL,   time_to = NULL,   station = NULL,   userid = NULL,   pwd = NULL,   save_csv = FALSE,   out_dir = "outputs" )

title: Get data of reflectance (level2) from WISPstation for a specific date — wisp_get_reflectance_data
text: version character. version API data. Default "1.0" time_from character. date time data requested. time_to character. date time data requested. station character.  name station. userid character. userid access data service. pwd character. password access data service. save_csv logical. TRUE, function saves reflectance data. out_dir character. directory CSV file saved. Default "outputs" within working directory.

title: Get data of reflectance (level2) from WISPstation for a specific date — wisp_get_reflectance_data
text: tibble measurement id, measurement date, instrument name, level2_quality, set sensor (irradiance radiances), waterquality values TSM, Chla, Kd, cpc provided instrument default, reflectance values 350 900 nm.

title: Get data of reflectance (level2) from WISPstation for a specific date — wisp_get_reflectance_data
text: Alessandro Oggioni, phD alessandro.oggioni@cnr.Nicola Ghirardi, phD nicola.ghirardi@cnr.

title: Get data of reflectance (level2) from WISPstation for a specific date — wisp_get_reflectance_data
code: # example code if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Not run: # NA data reflect_data <- wisp_get_reflectance_data(   time_from = "2024-09-01T09:00",   time_to = "2024-09-01T14:00",   station = "WISPstation012",   userid = userid,   pwd = pwd,   save_csv = FALSE,   out_dir = "outputs" )  # with data reflect_data <- wisp_get_reflectance_data(   time_from = "2024-08-01T09:00",   time_to = "2024-08-01T14:00",   station = "WISPstation012",   userid = userid,   pwd = pwd,   save_csv = FALSE,   out_dir = "outputs" )  # no data for the station selected reflect_data <- wisp_get_reflectance_data(   time_from = "2019-06-20T09:00",   time_to = "2019-06-20T14:00",   station = "WISPstation012",   userid = userid,   pwd = pwd,   save_csv = FALSE,   out_dir = "outputs" )  # The two dates are not consistent reflect_data <- wisp_get_reflectance_data(   time_from = "2019-06-20T09:00",   time_to = "2020-06-20T14:00",   station = "WISPstation012",   userid = userid,   pwd = pwd,   save_csv = FALSE,   out_dir = "outputs" ) } # } ## End (Not run)

what: Get data of reflectance (level2) from WISPstation for multiple dates — wisp_get_reflectance_multi_data
title: Get data of reflectance (level2) from WISPstation for multiple dates — wisp_get_reflectance_multi_data
text: function acts iterative wrapper around wisp_get_reflectance_data(). specifically designed download extended time series exceed limits single API request. function automatically splits user-defined time interval daily blocks, performs sequential downloads, aggregates retrieved data single, coherent tibble.

what: Calculation of SPM concentration (Novoa et al., 2017) — wisp_novoa_SPM
title: Calculation of SPM concentration (Novoa et al., 2017) — wisp_novoa_SPM
text: function calculates SPM concentration according Novoa et al., 2017 creates new columns qc_data e sr_data. calc_SPM can either TRUE (default) FALSE.

title: Calculation of SPM concentration (Novoa et al., 2017) — wisp_novoa_SPM
code: wisp_novoa_SPM(qc_data, sr_data, calc_SPM = TRUE)

title: Calculation of SPM concentration (Novoa et al., 2017) — wisp_novoa_SPM
text: qc_data tibble wisp_qc_reflectance_data() function. sr_data tibble wisp_sr_reflectance_data() function. calc_SPM logical. TRUE, function calculates NOVOA SPM concentrations. Default TRUE.

title: Calculation of SPM concentration (Novoa et al., 2017) — wisp_novoa_SPM
text: result list two elements: ...

title: Calculation of SPM concentration (Novoa et al., 2017) — wisp_novoa_SPM
text: Alessandro Oggioni, phD alessandro.oggioni@cnr.Nicola Ghirardi, phD nicola.ghirardi@cnr.

title: Calculation of SPM concentration (Novoa et al., 2017) — wisp_novoa_SPM
code: # example code if (FALSE) { #' ## Not run:  # Calculate NOVOA_SPM novoa_SPM_results <- WISP.data::wisp_novoa_SPM(qc_data = reflect_data_qc, sr_data = reflect_data_sr, calc_SPM = T) reflect_data_qc <- novoa_SPM_results$qc_data reflect_data_sr <- novoa_SPM_results$sr_data  # Don't calculate NOVOA_SPM novoa_SPM_results <- WISP.data::wisp_novoa_SPM(qc_data = reflect_data_qc, sr_data = reflect_data_sr, calc_SPM = F) reflect_data_qc <- novoa_SPM_results$qc_data reflect_data_sr <- novoa_SPM_results$sr_data } ## End (Not run)

what: Comparison plot of Raw vs QC vs SR reflectance data — wisp_plot_comparison
title: Comparison plot of Raw vs QC vs SR reflectance data — wisp_plot_comparison
text: function creates interactive side--side visual comparison different WISPstation data processing levels. Using plotly submodules, enables visualization three aligned plots within single interactive window: raw data downloaded directly WISPstation, processed data QC, processed data SR. provides powerful tool visually assessing filtering correction algorithms modify spectral signatures, remove artifacts, improve data quality.

title: Comparison plot of Raw vs QC vs SR reflectance data — wisp_plot_comparison
code: wisp_plot_comparison(   raw_data,   qc_data = NULL,   sr_data = NULL,   raw_args = NULL,   qc_args = NULL,   sr_args = NULL )

title: Comparison plot of Raw vs QC vs SR reflectance data — wisp_plot_comparison
text: raw_data tibble. original data obtained wisp_get_reflectance_data(). qc_data tibble. data wisp_qc_reflectance_data() operations. Default NULL. sr_data tibble. data wisp_sr_reflectance_data() operations. Default NULL. raw_args list arguments passed wisp_plot_reflectance_data raw data plot (legend). Default NULL. qc_args list arguments passed wisp_plot_reflectance_data QC data plot (legend). Default NULL. sr_args list arguments passed wisp_plot_reflectance_data SR data plot (legend). Default NULL.

title: Comparison plot of Raw vs QC vs SR reflectance data — wisp_plot_comparison
text: plotly subplot object comparing spectral signatures (Raw vs QC vs SR). raw_data provided valid, single plot returned.

title: Comparison plot of Raw vs QC vs SR reflectance data — wisp_plot_comparison
text: Alessandro Oggioni, phD alessandro.oggioni@cnr.Nicola Ghirardi, phD nicola.ghirardi@cnr.

title: Comparison plot of Raw vs QC vs SR reflectance data — wisp_plot_comparison
code: # example code if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Not run: custom_raw <- list(legend_TSM = FALSE, legend_Chla = FALSE) custom_qc <- list(legend_TSM = TRUE, legend_Chla = TRUE, legend_Kd = FALSE) custom_sr <- list(legend_TSM = TRUE, legend_mishra_CHL = FALSE)  wisp_plot_comparison(   raw_data = reflect_data,   qc_data  = reflect_data_qc,   sr_data  = reflect_data_sr,   raw_args = custom_raw,   qc_args  = custom_qc,   sr_args  = custom_sr ) print(fig_comparison) } # } ## End (Not run)

Default FALSE. legend_mishra_CHL logical. TRUE, plot legend includes Mishra_CHLvalues. Default FALSE. legend_hue_angle logical. TRUE, plot legend includes Hue_Anglevalues. Default FALSE. legend_dom_wavelength logical. TRUE, plot legend includes Dominant_Wavelengthvalues. Default FALSE. legend_OWT_class logical. TRUE, plot legend includes OWT_class. Default FALSE. legend_OWT_score logical. TRUE, plot legend includes OWT_score (membership grade). Default FALSE. legend_OWT_z_dist logical. TRUE, plot legend includes OWT_z_dist (statistical distance). Default FALSE.

what: Quality Control (QC) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_qc_reflectance_data
title: Quality Control (QC) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_qc_reflectance_data
text: function performs Quality Control (QC) process applies different algorithms spectral signatures. function applies structured sequence QC tests (QC1 - QC6), designed identify remove low-quality physically implausible spectra. addition, function integrates independent quality assessment metrics derived literature (QA QWIP), providing robust spectral validation established optical criteria.

title: Quality Control (QC) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_qc_reflectance_data
code: wisp_qc_reflectance_data(   data,   maxPeak = 0.05,   maxPeak_blue = 0.02,   qa_threshold = 0.5,   qwip_threshold = 0.2,   calc_scatt = TRUE,   calc_SPM = TRUE,   calc_TUR = TRUE,   calc_TSS = TRUE,   calc_gons = TRUE,   calc_gons740 = TRUE,   calc_NDCI = TRUE,   calc_mishra = TRUE,   calc_dom_wave = TRUE,   calc_OWT = TRUE,   save_csv = FALSE,   out_dir = "outputs" )

title: Quality Control (QC) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_qc_reflectance_data
text: data tibble. wisp_get_reflectance_data() function. maxPeak decimal. Maximum magnitude spectral signatures. recommend setting parameter : 0.02 clear oligotrophic water, 0.05 meso- eutrophic water, 0.08 hypereutrophic highly turbid water. Default 0.05. maxPeak_blue decimal. Maximum magnitude 350 nm values. recommend setting parameter : 0.02 (default). qa_threshold decimal. Minimum threshold Quality Assurance (QA). recommend setting parameter : 0.5 (default). make QC stringent, raise threshold. qwip_threshold decimal. Maximum threshold Quality Water Index Polynomial (QWIP). recommend setting parameter : 0.2 (default). make QC stringent, decrease threshold. calc_scatt logical. TRUE, function calculates peak due phytoplankton scattering (690-710 nm) ratio latter second chlorophyll absorption peak (670-680 nm). Default TRUE. calc_SPM logical. TRUE, function calculates SPM concentrations according Novoa et al., (2017). Default TRUE. calc_TUR logical. TRUE, function calculates turbidity (FNU) according Novoa et al., (2017). Default TRUE. calc_TSS logical. TRUE, function calculates TSS concentrations according Jiang et al., (2021). Default TRUE. calc_gons logical. TRUE, function calculates chlorophyll using Gons et al. (2002) algorithm (NIR ~782 nm). Default TRUE. calc_gons740 logical. TRUE, function calculates chlorophyll using Gons et al. (2002) algorithm (NIR ~740 nm). Default TRUE. calc_NDCI logical. TRUE, function calculates Normalised Difference Chlorophyll Index algorithm Mishra Mishra (2012). Default TRUE. calc_mishra logical. TRUE, function calculates chlorophyll using Mishra Mishra (2012) algorithm. Default TRUE. calc_dom_wave logical. TRUE, function calculates hue angle dominant wavelength. Default TRUE. calc_OWT logical. TRUE, function calculates Optical Water Type classification according Bi Hieronymi (2024) (OWT_class), membership probability (OWT_score), average Z-score (OWT_z_dist). addition, adds column description corresponding OWT class. "OWT_class" represents optical category analyzed spectral signature belongs based Gaussian Likelihood. "OWT_score" value 0 1 representing fuzzy membership grade; indicates probability belonging selected class relative available classes. "OWT_z_dist" indicates statistical distance observed spectrum class mean, weighted standard deviation. 0-1.5 indicate excellent fit OWT reference. Values 3 suggest spectral signature outlier deviates significantly typical range class. Default TRUE. save_csv logical. TRUE, function saves reflectance data. out_dir character. directory CSV file saved. Default "outputs" within working directory.

title: Quality Control (QC) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_qc_reflectance_data
text: tibble spectral signatures passed QC operation extra parameters requested. addition, message containing reason behind elimination anomalous spectral signature. parameter save_csv TRUE, function saves reflectance data CSV file.

title: Quality Control (QC) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_qc_reflectance_data
text: Alessandro Oggioni, phD alessandro.oggioni@cnr.Nicola Ghirardi, phD nicola.ghirardi@cnr.

title: Quality Control (QC) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_qc_reflectance_data
code: # example code if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Not run: reflect_data_qc <- wisp_qc_reflectance_data(   data = reflect_data,   maxPeak = 0.05,   maxPeak_blue = 0.02,   qa_threshold    = 0.5,   qwip_threshold  = 0.2,   calc_scatt = TRUE,   calc_SPM = TRUE,   calc_TUR = TRUE,   calc_TSS = TRUE,   calc_gons = TRUE,   calc_gons740 = TRUE,   calc_NDCI = TRUE,   calc_mishra = TRUE,   calc_dom_wave = TRUE,   calc_OWT = TRUE,   save_csv = FALSE,   out_dir = "outputs" ) } # } ## End (Not run)

what: Sky-glint Removal (SR) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_sr_reflectance_data
title: Sky-glint Removal (SR) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_sr_reflectance_data
text: function implements sky-glint Removal algorithm based methodology proposed Jiang et al. (2020). function operates spectra already filtered Quality Control process applies correction obtain glint-corrected remote sensing reflectance. Following correction, algorithms re-applied using corrected reflectance, ensuring accurate physically consistent estimates water constituents.

title: Sky-glint Removal (SR) for WISPstation reflectance data — wisp_sr_reflectance_data
code: wisp_sr_reflectance_data(   qc_data,   calc_scatt = TRUE,   calc_SPM = TRUE,   calc_TUR = TRUE,   calc_TSS = TRUE,   calc_gons = TRUE,   calc_gons740 = TRUE,   calc_NDCI = TRUE,   calc_mishra = TRUE,   calc_dom_wave = TRUE,   calc_OWT = TRUE,   save_csv = FALSE,   out_dir = "outputs" )

TRUE, function calculates SPM concentrations according Novoa et al., (2017). Default TRUE. calc_TUR logical. TRUE, function calculates turbidity (FNU) according Novoa et al., (2017). Default TRUE. calc_TSS logical. TRUE, function calculates TSS concentrations according Jiang et al., (2021). Default TRUE. calc_gons logical. TRUE, function calculates chlorophyll using Gons et al. (2002) algorithm (NIR ~782 nm). Default TRUE. calc_gons740 logical. TRUE, function calculates chlorophyll using Gons et al. (2002) algorithm (NIR ~740 nm). Default TRUE. calc_NDCI logical. TRUE, function calculates Normalised Difference Chlorophyll Index algorithm Mishra Mishra (2012). Default TRUE. calc_mishra logical. TRUE, function calculates chlorophyll using Mishra Mishra (2012) algorithm. Default TRUE. calc_dom_wave logical. TRUE, function calculates hue angle dominant wavelength. Default TRUE. calc_OWT logical. TRUE, function calculates Optical Water Type classification according Bi Hieronymi (2024) (OWT_class), membership probability (OWT_score), average Z-score (OWT_z_dist). addition, adds column description corresponding OWT class. "OWT_class" represents optical category analyzed spectral signature belongs based Gaussian Likelihood. "OWT_score" value 0 1 representing fuzzy membership grade; indicates probability belonging selected class relative available classes. "OWT_z_dist" indicates statistical distance observed spectrum class mean, weighted standard deviation. 0-1.5 indicate excellent fit OWT reference. Values 3 suggest spectral signature outlier deviates significantly typical range class. Default TRUE. save_csv logical. what: Create a temporal trend plot of one or more parameters — wisp_trend_plot
title: Create a temporal trend plot of one or more parameters — wisp_trend_plot
text: function generates interactive temporal plots one parameters. designed handle high-frequency measurements within single day long-term time series spanning multiple months. function can aggregate data (daily mean median) allows comparison multiple parameters plot share unit measurement.

title: Create a temporal trend plot of one or more parameters — wisp_trend_plot
code: wisp_trend_plot(   data,   params = c("TSM", "Chla"),   datetime_col = "measurement.date",   instrument_col = "instrument.name",   aggregate = c("none", "daily_mean", "daily_median"),   merge_plot = FALSE,   na.rm = TRUE,   colors = NULL,   title = NULL,   return_long_df = FALSE )

title: Create a temporal trend plot of one or more parameters — wisp_trend_plot
text: data tibble containing water quality parameters spectral signatures. params character vector specifying parameters plot. Default c("TSM", "Chla"). datetime_col character. Name column datetime values. Default "measurement.date". instrument_col character. Name column instrument identifiers. Default "instrument.name". aggregate character specifying whether aggregate data. Options : "none": Plots available values (requires data one day). "daily_mean": Calculates plots daily average, including ribbon Standard Deviation (SD) (requires data multiple days). "daily_median": Calculates plots daily median (requires data multiple days). Default "none". merge_plot logical. TRUE, parameters share unit measurement merged single plot. function throw error common units found among requested params. Default FALSE. na.rm logical. TRUE, NA values ignored aggregation. Default TRUE. colors character vector colors parameter. Default uses viridis palette. title character. Optional title plot. Default NULL. return_long_df logical. TRUE, function returns long format dataframe used plotting instead plotly object. Default FALSE.

title: Create a temporal trend plot of one or more parameters — wisp_trend_plot
text: interactive plotly object showing temporal trend selected parameters, optional ribbons standard deviation.

title: Create a temporal trend plot of one or more parameters — wisp_trend_plot
text: Alessandro Oggioni, PhD alessandro.oggioni@cnr.Nicola Ghirardi, PhD nicola.ghirardi@cnr.

title: Create a temporal trend plot of one or more parameters — wisp_trend_plot
code: # Example usage if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Standard plot with facets for each parameter fig_trend <- wisp_trend_plot(    data = reflect_data_sr,    params = c("TSM", "Chla"),    aggregate = "none",    merge_plot = FALSE ) print(fig_trend)  # Merged plot for parameters with common units  fig_merged <- wisp_trend_plot(    data = reflect_data_sr,    params = c("TSM", "Novoa_SPM"),    aggregate = "daily_mean",    merge_plot = TRUE ) print(fig_trend) } # }
